name: Parallax Scroll
summary: A three-column image grid with scroll-based parallax where columns move at different vertical speeds.
detailedDescription: Parallax Scroll creates a scrollable image gallery with three columns that move at different rates during scroll. The component uses Framer Motion's useScroll hook tracking container scroll from 'start start' to 'end start'. Three transform values are created: first column translates -200px (upward), second column translates +200px (downward), third column translates -200px (upward). Images are divided equally into three parts using Math.ceil(length/3). Each column applies its transform via motion.div style prop. Images display as 320px tall with object-cover and object-left-top positioning for consistent cropping. The grid uses responsive breakpoints (1 col mobile, 2 cols tablet, 3 cols desktop) with 10-unit gaps and generous padding (py-40, px-10). Container has fixed 40rem height with overflow-y-auto for scroll behavior. This creates a dynamic gallery where images appear to float at different speeds, adding depth and visual interest to portfolios, product showcases, or any image-heavy section.
purpose: Display image galleries with differential scroll speeds
Create parallax depth through varied column movement
Showcase images with engaging scroll interaction
behavior: Applies different Y translations per column
Divides images into three equal groups
First and third columns move up, middle moves down
Uses object-cover for consistent image cropping
visualCharacteristics: Outer columns move up on scroll
recommendations: Use where moderate motion and reusable composition improve UX and clarity.
